assets.title: Get oriented in a new repository
assets.description: Describe what you want to know, not which files to read. Claude explores the project on its own and returns a summary of how it fits together.
assets.prompt: give me an overview of this codebase: architecture, key directories, and how the pieces connect

assets.title: Explain unfamiliar code
assets.description: Name the file and say what format you want the answer in. Swap the HTML page for a diagram, bullet points, or whatever fits how you learn.
assets.prompt: explain what {path} does and how data flows through it. write it up as {format}

slots.format: an HTML page with a diagram, then open it in my browser

assets.title: Find where something happens
assets.description: Search by behavior instead of by filename. The search works even when you don't know what the file is called or which directory it lives in.
assets.prompt: where do we {behavior}?

assets.title: Check what breaks before you delete
assets.description: Ask before you remove anything. The list of callers and downstream effects tells you whether you're looking at a one-line cleanup or a change you need to coordinate.
assets.prompt: what would break if I deleted {target}?

assets.title: Trace how code evolved
assets.description: Point at commit history when the question is why, not what. Claude reads the log and blame for whatever version control you use and explains the decisions behind the current implementation.
assets.prompt: look through the commit history of {path} and summarize how it evolved and why

assets.title: Scope a change before you start
assets.description: Size the work before you commit it to a roadmap. The file list tells you whether you're looking at one component or a cross-cutting change.
assets.prompt: which files would I need to touch to {change}?

slots.change: add a dark mode toggle to settings

assets.title: Ask the codebase a product question
assets.description: State your role so the answer is pitched at the right level. Claude explains what the product actually does from the source code, without you needing to read it.
assets.prompt: I am a {role}. walk me through what happens when a user {action}, from the UI down to the result

assets.title: Plan a multi-file change before touching code
assets.description: Adding "don't edit yet" separates exploration from changes, so you see the approach before any code moves. To make plan-first the default on every prompt, press Shift+Tab for plan mode.
assets.prompt: plan how to refactor the {target} to {goal}. list the files you would change, but don't edit anything yet

assets.title: Draft a spec by interview
assets.description: Ask to be interviewed instead of writing the spec yourself. Claude asks you structured questions until the requirements are complete, then writes the result to a file.
assets.prompt: I want to build {feature}. interview me about implementation, UX, edge cases, and tradeoffs until we have covered everything, then write the spec to SPEC.md

assets.title: Turn a meeting into tickets
assets.description: Skip the transcription step. Claude pulls action items from the unstructured input and writes them straight into your tracker via MCP, so you review the tickets, not the transcript.
assets.prompt: read {input} and write up the action items, then create a {tracker} ticket for each with acceptance criteria

assets.title: Map edge cases before building
assets.description: Ask for what's missing, not what's there. Claude lists the error states, empty states, and edge cases a happy-path design tends to skip.
assets.prompt: list the error states, empty states, and edge cases for {feature} that the design needs to cover

assets.title: Turn a mockup into a working prototype
assets.description: A clickable prototype answers questions a static mockup can't. Hand the working code to engineering instead of explaining the interactions in a doc.
assets.prompt: here is a mockup. build a working prototype I can click through, matching the layout and states shown

assets.title: Implement from a screenshot and self-check
assets.description: This gives Claude a verification loop: it renders, compares against the source image, and iterates without you pointing out each gap.
assets.prompt: implement this design, then take a screenshot of the result, compare it to the original, and fix any differences

assets.title: Follow an existing pattern
assets.description: Point at code you already like. Without a reference, Claude defaults to general best practices. With one, it matches the conventions your codebase actually uses.
assets.prompt: look at how {example} is implemented to understand the pattern, then build {new} the same way

assets.title: Generate docs for undocumented code
assets.description: Name the scope and the format. Claude finds what's missing and matches the comment style already in the file, so the new docs read like the rest.
assets.prompt: find {scope} without {format} comments and add them, matching the style already used in the file

assets.title: Add a small, well-defined feature
assets.description: State the inputs and outputs, not how to build it. Claude finds where similar code lives and adds yours alongside it.
assets.prompt: add a {endpoint} endpoint that returns {payload}

assets.title: Build a small internal tool from scratch
assets.description: You don't need a project, a framework, or a build step. Describe the tool and ask Claude to open it so you see it working immediately.
assets.prompt: create a {tool} using HTML, CSS, and vanilla JavaScript, then open it in my browser

slots.tool: drag-and-drop Kanban board with three columns

assets.title: Work an issue end to end
assets.description: Give the issue number, not a summary. Claude reads the full ticket itself, so requirements you'd forget to mention come through, and it validates the change before reporting back.
assets.prompt: read issue #{issue}, implement the fix, and run the tests

assets.title: Find and update copy across the codebase
assets.description: Ask for variants and say what to skip. Claude finds phrasings a literal search would miss and leaves test fixtures and history untouched, so you review only the copy users actually see.
assets.prompt: find every place we say "{copy}" or a close variant, show me each one in context, then update them all to "{new}". leave tests and the changelog alone

assets.title: Draft a document from past examples
assets.description: Point at a folder of finished work instead of describing your style. Claude learns the structure and voice from what you've already shipped, so the first draft reads like one of yours.
assets.prompt: read the {examples} in {folder} to learn the structure and voice, then draft a new one for {topic}

assets.title: Write tests, run them, fix failures
assets.description: Ask for write, run, and fix together so Claude iterates without stopping for instructions.
assets.prompt: write tests for {path}, run them, and fix any failures

assets.title: Drive implementation from tests
assets.description: Test-driven development: the tests define when the work is complete, and Claude iterates on the implementation until they pass.
assets.prompt: write tests for {feature} first, then implement it until they pass

assets.title: Fill gaps from a coverage report
assets.description: Point at the coverage report instead of guessing what's untested. Claude reads the actual numbers and writes tests for the files that need them most.
assets.prompt: read {report} and add tests for the lowest-covered files until each is above {target}%

assets.title: Migrate a pattern across the codebase
assets.description: Describe the old pattern and the new one. Asking Claude to identify every place first means the call sites are listed in the response, so you can check none were missed.
assets.prompt: migrate everything from {from} to {to}: identify every place that needs to change, then make the changes

assets.title: Port code to another language
assets.description: Say what to preserve, not just the target language. Naming the API or behavior that must stay the same gives Claude a contract to check the port against.
assets.prompt: port {source} to {target}, keeping the same {keep}

assets.title: Optimize against a measurable target
assets.description: Stating the metric and target gives Claude a clear definition of done.
assets.prompt: optimize {target} to bring {metric} from {current} down to under {goal}

assets.title: Fix a precise visual bug
assets.description: Precise visual feedback gets a precise fix. State the exact element, measurement, and viewport.
assets.prompt: the {element} extends {amount} beyond the {container} on {viewport}. fix it.

assets.title: Review your changes before you commit
assets.description: Catch problems while they're still cheap to fix. Claude reads the changed files in full, not just the diff lines, so it spots issues a quick self-review misses.
assets.prompt: review my uncommitted changes and flag anything that looks risky before I commit

assets.title: Review a pull request
assets.description: Claude reviews with the whole codebase in context, not just the diff. It reads the changed code and what it calls, so it catches problems a diff-only review would miss.
assets.prompt: review PR #{pr} and summarize what changed, then list any concerns

assets.title: Review infrastructure changes before applying
assets.description: Plan output is dense and hard to scan. Pasting it gets you a plain-language summary of what's actually going to change before you apply it.
assets.prompt: here is my Terraform plan output. what is this going to do, and is anything here going to cause problems?

assets.title: Run a security review with a subagent
assets.description: A subagent runs the audit in its own context window and reports back a summary, so a long security review doesn't fill up your main session. The built-in general-purpose subagent handles this without extra setup.
assets.prompt: use a subagent to review {path} for security issues and report what it finds

assets.title: Catch issues before formal review
assets.description: Get a first pass before a human spends time on it. Name the concerns you want checked so the review is focused, then fix what it finds and send a cleaner draft.
assets.prompt: review {file} for {concerns} and list anything I should fix before it goes to {reviewer}

slots.concerns: unsupported claims, missing attributions, and brand-guideline issues

assets.title: Course-correct a wrong approach
assets.description: Name the constraint Claude missed, not just that it's wrong. A specific reason gives Claude a concrete constraint to satisfy on the retry, instead of guessing again.
assets.prompt: that is not right: {feedback}. try a different approach

slots.feedback: the function signature needs to stay backward-compatible

assets.title: Narrow the scope of a change
assets.description: When the direction is right but the change went too broad, ask Claude to keep part of it rather than rewinding everything. A stated boundary keeps a small fix from becoming a refactor.
assets.prompt: that is too much. keep only the changes to {scope} and undo your other edits

assets.title: Turn a correction into a rule
assets.description: A correction in chat isn't shared with your team. A rule in the project's CLAUDE.md is shared once you commit it, and Claude reads it at the start of every session.
assets.prompt: you keep {mistake}. add a rule to CLAUDE.md so this stops happening

slots.mistake: using default exports when this project uses named exports

assets.title: Resolve merge conflicts
assets.description: Say what state you want, not which markers to keep. Asking for the reasoning makes the merge reviewable instead of a black box.
assets.prompt: resolve the merge conflicts in this branch and explain what you kept from each side

assets.title: Commit with a generated message
assets.description: Let Claude derive the message from the diff. It matches your repository's existing commit style.
assets.prompt: commit these changes with a message that summarizes what I did

assets.title: Open a pull request from a ticket
assets.description: Skip the context switch between tracker, editor, and GitHub. One prompt reads the spec, makes the change, and opens the PR.
assets.prompt: find the {tracker} ticket about {topic} and open a PR that implements it

assets.title: Draft release notes from git history
assets.description: Give two reference points and the structure you want. Claude reads the commit log between them and drafts a changelog you can edit.
assets.prompt: compare {from} to {to} and draft release notes grouped by feature, fix, and breaking change

assets.title: Write a CI workflow
assets.description: Describe when it should run and what it should do; the YAML is generated for you, matched to your project's build and test commands.
assets.prompt: write a GitHub Actions workflow that {steps} on every push to {branch}

slots.steps: runs the tests and deploys to staging

assets.title: Find and fix a failing test
assets.description: Describe the symptom; you don't need to know which file is broken. Claude runs the test to see the failure, traces it into source, and fixes it.
assets.prompt: the {test} test is failing, find out why and fix it

assets.title: Investigate a reported error
assets.description: Describe the symptom and location; Claude reads the relevant code path and traces likely causes. Paste stack traces or logs if you have them.
assets.prompt: users are seeing {symptom} on {where}. investigate and tell me what is going on

assets.title: Fix a build error at the root
assets.description: Asking for root cause and verification prevents surface-level patches that suppress the error without fixing it.
assets.prompt: here is a build error. fix the root cause and verify the build succeeds

assets.title: Investigate a production incident
assets.description: List the evidence sources to correlate, not the steps to take. Claude reads logs, git history, and config together to narrow the cause.
assets.prompt: {symptom}. check the logs, recent deploys, and config changes, then tell me the most likely cause

slots.symptom: the checkout endpoint started returning 500s an hour ago

assets.title: Diagnose from a console screenshot
assets.description: Cloud consoles show you the problem but not the commands to fix it. Claude reads the screenshot and translates the dashboard into the kubectl, gcloud, or aws commands to run.
assets.prompt: here is a screenshot of {console}. walk me through why {resource} is failing and give me the exact commands to fix it

assets.title: Query logs in plain English
assets.description: Ask the question instead of writing the SQL. Claude builds the query, runs it against your connected logs, and shows both the query and the result so you can check what ran.
assets.prompt: show me all {events} for {scope} over {timeframe}. write the query, run it, and tell me what stands out

assets.title: Analyze a data file
assets.description: A one-off question doesn't need a one-off script. Point at a file in your project folder and Claude reads it directly, finds the patterns, and writes the output where you ask.
assets.prompt: read {file}, summarize the key patterns, and write the results to {output}

slots.output: an HTML page with charts, then open it in my browser

assets.title: Generate variations from performance data
assets.description: State the constraint at the start so generation stays within the limit. Claude reads the metrics, picks what to replace, and produces alternatives that fit.
assets.prompt: read {file}, find the underperforming {items}, and generate {n} new variations that stay under {limit} characters

assets.title: Turn a recurring task into a skill
assets.description: Name the steps once; reuse them as a command. Claude writes a skill anyone on your team can run.
assets.prompt: create a /{name} skill for this project that {steps}

slots.name: ship
slots.steps: runs the linter and tests, then drafts a commit message

assets.title: Add a hook for repeat behavior
assets.description: Hooks make a behavior automatic instead of something you have to remember to ask for. Describe the trigger and action and Claude writes the hook configuration.
assets.prompt: write a hook that {action} after every {event}

assets.title: Connect a tool with MCP
assets.description: Connect the source once instead of pasting data every session. After MCP setup, Claude reads from the tool directly when you ask about it.
assets.prompt: set up the {server} MCP server so you can read my {data} directly

assets.title: Capture what to remember for next time
assets.description: Ask before you forget. Claude knows what it had to figure out this session and proposes CLAUDE.md entries so the next session starts with that context.
assets.prompt: summarize what we did this session and suggest what to add to CLAUDE.md
